exercises.title: What does Feign generate?
exercises.scenario: Feign generates a proxy implementing the declared interface.
exercises.expected_solution:
Spring generates: a proxy class implementing UserServiceClient
Spring registers it as a Spring bean (injectable via constructor)

userServiceClient.getUser('U001'):
→ HTTP GET http://localhost:8081/api/users/U001
→ Deserializes response JSON to UserDto

userServiceClient.createUser(request):
→ HTTP POST http://localhost:8081/api/users
→ Body: {"name":"Aisha","email":"aisha@example.com"} (Jackson serialized)
→ Deserializes response JSON to UserDto

test_cases.expected:
Spring generates: a proxy class implementing UserServiceClient
Spring registers it as a Spring bean (injectable via constructor)

userServiceClient.getUser('U001'):
→ HTTP GET http://localhost:8081/api/users/U001
→ Deserializes response JSON to UserDto

userServiceClient.createUser(request):
→ HTTP POST http://localhost:8081/api/users
→ Body: {"name":"Aisha","email":"aisha@example.com"} (Jackson serialized)
→ Deserializes response JSON to UserDto

hints: Feign generates a Spring bean implementing the interface
@PathVariable: substituted into the URL path
@RequestBody: serialized as JSON request body

exercises.title: Complete a @FeignClient interface
exercises.scenario: Feign interfaces use Spring MVC annotations.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to declare a complete inventory service client.

blanks.hint: Annotation that declares this as a Feign client
The request body serialized as JSON

hints: @FeignClient: marks interface as a generated HTTP client
Same annotations as @RestController, but from the caller's side

exercises.title: Minimal @FeignClient declaration
hints: @EnableFeignClients: scans for @FeignClient interfaces and generates proxies
void return type: for DELETE that returns no body

exercises.title: Feign with @RequestParam — query string parameters
exercises.scenario: @RequestParam in Feign builds the query string automatically.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `SearchServiceClient` @FeignClient with:
1. `searchProducts(String query, String category, int page, int size)` → GET /api/search/products
2. `searchOrders(String userId, String status, String fromDate)` where fromDate is optional
3. Show how query params become the URL: search('mouse', 'electronics', 0, 20) → /api/search/products?query=mouse&category=electronics&page=0&size=20
hints: @RequestParam(defaultValue='0'): optional with default
@RequestParam(required=false): null means param omitted from URL
Feign builds the query string automatically from @RequestParam

exercises.title: Inject @FeignClient into a @Service
exercises.scenario: Feign clients are injected like any Spring bean.
exercises.instructions:
Write `TradeOrderService` that:
1. Constructor-injects `MarketServiceClient` (a @FeignClient interface)
2. `executeTrade(userId, symbol, qty)` calls marketClient.getQuote(symbol) and marketClient.reserveShares(symbol, qty) — use Feign
3. Returns a TradeConfirmation record

The service never uses RestTemplate — only the Feign interface.
hints: MarketServiceClient is injected like any Spring bean — via constructor
marketClient.getQuote(): looks local, actually makes HTTP GET
No URL building, no try-catch inside the service method

exercises.title: Custom ErrorDecoder — map HTTP to domain exceptions
exercises.scenario: ErrorDecoder translates HTTP status codes to domain exceptions once.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `PaymentServiceErrorDecoder` implementing `feign.codec.ErrorDecoder` that:
1. 404 → PaymentNotFoundException
2. 402 (Payment Required) → InsufficientFundsException
3. 409 → DuplicatePaymentException
4. 503 → PaymentServiceUnavailableException
5. other → RuntimeException with status code

Register in @Configuration.
hints: ErrorDecoder.decode(methodKey, response): called for all non-2xx responses
response.status(): the HTTP status code as int
switch(status) with int: Java 14+ enhanced switch or traditional switch

exercises.title: Test Feign with @MockBean
exercises.scenario: In @WebMvcTest, replace the Feign client with a Mockito mock.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @WebMvcTest for `TradeController` that:
1. @MockBean the MarketServiceClient (Feign interface)
2. Mocks getQuote() to return a successful quote
3. Tests POST /api/trades → 201
4. Tests POST /api/trades when getQuote() throws SymbolNotFoundException → 404
hints: @MockBean MarketServiceClient: Mockito mock replaces the Feign proxy in @WebMvcTest
Mock the service layer, not the Feign client — the controller calls the service
SymbolNotFoundException → 404 via @ExceptionHandler in @RestControllerAdvice

exercises.title: Test Feign with WireMock
exercises.scenario: WireMock starts a real HTTP stub — tests the Feign client itself.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @SpringBootTest test using WireMock that tests `UserServiceClient` @FeignClient directly:
1. Start WireMock on a random port
2. Stub GET /api/users/U001 → 200 with user JSON
3. Stub GET /api/users/UNKNOWN → 404
4. Assert client.getUser('U001') returns correct user
5. Assert client.getUser('UNKNOWN') throws the mapped exception
hints: WireMockServer(wireMockConfig().port(8099)): stub HTTP server on port 8099
@TestPropertySource(properties='user.service.url=http://localhost:8099'): point Feign to WireMock
okJson(jsonString): shorthand for 200 OK with JSON body

exercises.title: @RequestHeader — pass authentication token in Feign
exercises.scenario: Feign can pass custom headers on every call.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `PortfolioServiceClient` @FeignClient that:
1. `getPortfolio(String userId)` → GET /api/portfolio/{userId} + @RequestHeader 'X-Auth-Token'
2. `getHoldings(String userId, String symbol)` → GET with both path variable and auth header
3. Write a `PortfolioService` that gets the auth token from SecurityContext and passes it to Feign
hints: @RequestHeader('X-Auth-Token'): Feign adds this as an HTTP header on the call
SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getCredentials(): JWT string
Alternatively: use a RequestInterceptor to automatically add headers (cleaner)

exercises.title: What happens when Feign gets 500?
exercises.scenario: Unconfigured Feign error handling bubbles up differently.
exercises.expected_solution:
Feign throws: FeignException.InternalServerError (subclass of FeignException)
with message: '[500] during [GET] to [http://localhost:8083/api/stocks/TCS]'

Your API: returns 500 Internal Server Error
(FeignException bubbles up, unhandled, becomes 500)

Fix: add ErrorDecoder OR catch FeignException in service layer OR add @ExceptionHandler(FeignException.class) in @ControllerAdvice

test_cases.expected:
Feign throws: FeignException.InternalServerError (subclass of FeignException)
with message: '[500] during [GET] to [http://localhost:8083/api/stocks/TCS]'

Your API: returns 500 Internal Server Error
(FeignException bubbles up, unhandled, becomes 500)

Fix: add ErrorDecoder OR catch FeignException in service layer OR add @ExceptionHandler(FeignException.class) in @ControllerAdvice

hints: Without ErrorDecoder: Feign throws FeignException for any non-2xx
FeignException bubbles up through Spring MVC as an unhandled exception → 500
ErrorDecoder translates HTTP errors to domain exceptions before they escape

exercises.title: Feign timeout configuration
exercises.scenario: Set per-client connection and read timeouts.
exercises.instructions:
Write the application.properties configuration for:
1. Default timeout: connect=3s, read=5s
2. payment-service: connect=2s, read=10s (payments can be slow)
3. market-service: connect=1s, read=2s (must be fast for trading)
4. Enable FULL logging for payment-service (dev)

Also show a @Configuration approach using `Request.Options`.
exercises.starter_code:
# application.properties — Feign timeout configuration

// Also show @Configuration approach:
hints: spring.cloud.openfeign.client.config.{client-name}: per-client config
connect-timeout: time to establish TCP connection
read-timeout: time to wait for response after connection established

exercises.title: Feign RequestInterceptor — auto-inject auth token
exercises.scenario: A RequestInterceptor adds headers to ALL calls from a Feign client.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `JwtRequestInterceptor` implementing `feign.RequestInterceptor` that:
1. Reads JWT from the current SecurityContext
2. Adds 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' header to every Feign call
3. Register it as @Bean applied to ALL Feign clients
4. Write @SpringBootTest verifying the header is sent (use WireMock to capture headers)
hints: RequestInterceptor: lambda (template) -> template.header('key','value')
@Bean in @Configuration: applies to ALL @FeignClient instances
WireMock withHeader(): asserts that the header was sent by Feign

exercises.title: Feign client with full error handling — Razorpay style
exercises.scenario: Production Feign client with error decoder, timeout, and @SpringBootTest.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete Razorpay-style payment Feign client:
1. `PaymentServiceClient` @FeignClient with 3 methods: createPayment, getPayment, refundPayment
2. `PaymentErrorDecoder`: 402→InsufficientFundsException, 404→PaymentNotFoundException, 409→DuplicatePaymentException, 503→PaymentServiceDownException
3. `PaymentFeignConfig` @Configuration with error decoder + 10s read timeout
4. @SpringBootTest + WireMock: test all 4 error scenarios
hints: configuration=PaymentFeignConfig.class: apply config to this client only
WireMock stub: get('/api/v1/payments/X').willReturn(aResponse().withStatus(404))
All 4 tests use different WireMock stubs and assert different exceptions

exercises.title: Multiple Feign clients in one service
exercises.scenario: A service often calls multiple downstream services.
exercises.instructions:
Write an `OrderOrchestrationService` that injects 3 Feign clients:
- `UserServiceClient`: verifyUser(userId)
- `InventoryServiceClient`: checkAndReserve(productId, qty)
- `PaymentServiceClient`: charge(userId, amount)

The method orchestrates these 3 calls. Write @SpringBootTest with 3 WireMock servers (or one WireMock with different URL paths). Test: success, user invalid, inventory insufficient, payment declined.
hints: One WireMock server handles all clients pointing to localhost:9500
urlPathEqualTo: matches path only (ignores query params)
Helper methods (stubUser, stubInventory, stubPayment) reduce duplication

exercises.title: Complete Feign service — Topic 15.2 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full production Feign stack: client, config, error decoder, WireMock tests.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete Zerodha-style portfolio Feign service:
1. `HoldingServiceClient` @FeignClient: getHoldings(userId), updateHolding(holding), deleteHolding(id)
2. `MarketDataClient` @FeignClient: getCurrentPrices(List<String> symbols) → Map<String, Double>
3. `PortfolioService`: getPortfolioValue(userId) = sum of holding.qty * currentPrice for each symbol
4. Custom ErrorDecoder for HoldingServiceClient (404→HoldingNotFoundException, 503→ServiceDownException)
5. RequestInterceptor adds X-Request-Id header (UUID per request) to all Feign calls
6. 4 WireMock @SpringBootTest tests: value calculated, holding not found, service down, market prices fetched

All 4 tests pass.
exercises.starter_code:
// Complete portfolio Feign stack
// 2 Feign clients + PortfolioService + ErrorDecoder + RequestInterceptor + 4 tests
hints: RequestInterceptor as @Bean: applies to ALL Feign clients in the context
urlPathEqualTo: ignores query params — safe for ?userId=U01
Empty holdings list: short-circuit before calling market-data
